name: 经济学文献雷达

topics.name: AI与公司金融、劳动经济学
topics.scope: Empirical papers on AI adoption and firm value, financing, investment, innovation, productivity, workforce composition, hiring, wages, tasks, employment, credit allocation, banking, or risk.
exclude: AI ethics without firm finance or labor outcomes
creator-platform effects unless directly about firm labor demand

topics.name: 企业灵活用工与组织边界
topics.scope: Firm-side labor arrangements only: why firms use outsourcing, labor dispatch, staffing agencies, temporary contracts, subcontracting, contingent workers, franchising, and external service providers; prioritize effects on firm productivity, costs, innovation, risk, governance, wages, and employment structure.
topics.include_if: The paper must center on a firm's labor sourcing, contracting, staffing, workforce-boundary, or employment-structure decision, or measure its effects on firms and workers.
exclude: remote work or work from home unless the central question is the firm's staffing or organizational-boundary decision
platform workers or creator economy without a clear client-firm staffing decision
self-employment trends without firm labor demand

english_journals: Journal of Economic Behavior and Organization
chinese_journals: 数量经济技术经济研究

rules: Prioritize 8-12 high-quality papers on enterprise-side flexible staffing: outsourcing, labor dispatch, staffing agencies, temporary or fixed-term contracts, subcontracting, contingent workforces, and firm-boundary decisions.

Do not treat remote work, individual platform labor, creator economy, or self-employment as enterprise flexible staffing unless the central research question is the client firm's staffing, contracting, or organizational-boundary decision.
AI papers must be directly related to corporate finance, banking, firm productivity or innovation, labor demand, hiring, wages, tasks, skills, workforce composition, or employment.
Exclude facial/personality inference and generic AI application papers that do not study corporate-finance or labor-economics outcomes.

Prefer empirical papers. The normal daily list should contain only empirical or experimental papers; include theory only when the user explicitly requests it or when it is indispensable and clearly labeled.
Every selected paper should receive the full structured deep-summary format; do not split the daily list into detailed and brief cards.
For each paper, clearly separate research motivation, research background, data source, innovation, concrete paper content, mechanism, findings, relevance, and evidence limits.
Do not fabricate papers, authors, links, abstracts, or findings.
If only title or metadata is available, mark evidence as limited and avoid inventing conclusions.
